title: Get started with blueterra
text: bathy_info() first check raster dimensions, value range, resolution, extent, CRS. fields affect slope, focal-window metrics, buffers, distance along transects.

title: Get started with blueterra
text: example rasters store bathymetry negative elevation. prepare_bathy() preserves convention unless conversion requested explicitly.  Hillshade used visual relief. helps reader see escarpments local relief, predictor unless analyst explicitly derives includes .
code: prepared <- prepare_bathy(   hitw,   depth_range = c(-220, -25),   smooth = TRUE,   smooth_window = 3 )  check_bathy_units(prepared, units = "m", positive_depth = FALSE) #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   layer     min   max units positive_depth #>   <chr>   <dbl> <dbl> <chr> <lgl>          #> 1 bathy_m -218. -27.2 m     FALSE range(terra::values(prepared), na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] -217.94669  -27.22529 plot_bathy(   prepared,   contours = TRUE,   contour_interval = 25,   vectors = hitw_rect,   title = "Hole-in-the-Wall Bathymetry",   subtitle = "Hillshade, contours, and sampling rectangle" )

title: Get started with blueterra
text: Terrain metrics describe different aspects bathymetric surface. Slope aspect describe local orientation; northness eastness convert aspect linear components; TRI rugosity describe local relief variability; BPI describes relative position; curvature summarizes local surface bending.  Process groups keep related derivatives together. interpretation categories terrain form, direct measurements currents, sediment transport, habitat condition, ecological response.
code: terrain <- derive_terrain(   prepared,   metrics = c(     "slope", "aspect", "northness", "eastness", "tri", "rugosity",     "bpi", "curvature", "surface_area_ratio"   ) )  names(terrain) #>  [1] "slope_deg"          "aspect_deg"         "northness"          #>  [4] "eastness"           "tri"                "rugosity_vrm_3x3"   #>  [7] "bpi_3x3"            "bpi_11x11"          "curvature"          #> [10] "surface_area_ratio" assign_process_groups(terrain) #> # A tibble: 10 × 7 #>    metric        metric_standard label process_group description source_function #>    <chr>         <chr>           <chr> <chr>         <chr>       <chr>           #>  1 slope_deg     slope_deg       Slope slope_gradie… Local stee… derive_slope    #>  2 aspect_deg    aspect_deg      Aspe… seafloor_asp… Local down… derive_aspect   #>  3 northness     northness       Nort… seafloor_asp… Cosine tra… derive_northne… #>  4 eastness      eastness        East… seafloor_asp… Sine trans… derive_eastness #>  5 tri           tri             Terr… seafloor_rug… Local terr… derive_tri      #>  6 rugosity_vrm… rugosity_vrm_3… Vect… seafloor_rug… Vector rug… derive_rugosity #>  7 bpi_3x3       bpi_3x3         Fine… seafloor_pos… Fine-scale… derive_bpi      #>  8 bpi_11x11     bpi_11x11       Broa… seafloor_pos… Broad-scal… derive_bpi      #>  9 curvature     curvature       Curv… curvature     Laplacian-… derive_curvatu… #> 10 surface_area… surface_area_r… Surf… seafloor_rug… Approximat… derive_surface… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: matched <lgl> summarize_process_groups(terrain) #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   process_group     n_metrics metrics                                   #>   <chr>                 <int> <chr>                                     #> 1 curvature                 1 curvature                                 #> 2 seafloor_aspect           3 aspect_deg, northness, eastness           #> 3 seafloor_position         2 bpi_3x3, bpi_11x11                        #> 4 seafloor_rugosity         3 tri, rugosity_vrm_3x3, surface_area_ratio #> 5 slope_gradient            1 slope_deg plot_metric_stack(terrain[[c("slope_deg", "tri", "bpi_3x3", "curvature")]])

title: Get started with blueterra
text: Sampling rectangles provide compact example zone-based extraction. polygon treated spatial sampling frame, summary statistics calculated raster cells inside frame.
code: zone_summary <- summarize_terrain(   terrain,   hitw_rect,   fun = c("mean", "sd", "min", "max") )  zone_summary[, c("site_id", "site_name", "slope_deg_mean", "bpi_3x3_mean")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   site_id site_name        slope_deg_mean bpi_3x3_mean #>   <chr>   <chr>                     <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 hitw    Hole-in-the-Wall           50.9      0.00568

title: Get started with blueterra
text: Transects convert raster surface profiles. bathy supplied, make_transects() can estimate terrain-oriented line angle local aspect instead requiring analyst choose fixed direction hand.   y-axis explicitly set bathy_m. prevents transect metadata width, angle, offset mistaken raster value. Distance oriented top shallow endpoint toward bottom deeper endpoint, plotted distance reset zero trimming empty profile ends. mean cross-section averaged interpolated transects missing interpolated values removed default, longer profiles still contribute beyond shortest transect.  Metric profiles can use transect geometry. bathymetry column metric column present, plot_depth_profile() places metric x-axis bathymetry depth y-axis. useful showing terrain attributes change along depth gradient.
code: orientation <- estimate_surface_orientation(prepared, hitw_rect) transects <- make_transects(hitw_rect, spacing = 75, bathy = prepared) cross_sections <- sample_transects(transects, prepared, n = 12)  orientation #> [1] 94.61515 unique(as.data.frame(transects)[, c("angle_deg", "angle_source", "mean_aspect_deg")]) #>   angle_deg angle_source mean_aspect_deg #> 1  94.61515      surface        175.3849 head(cross_sections[, c("transect_id", "distance", "bathy_m")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   transect_id distance bathy_m #>   <chr>          <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 1_1              0     NaN   #> 2 1_1             27.4   NaN   #> 3 1_1             54.7   NaN   #> 4 1_1             82.1  -197.  #> 5 1_1            109.    -94.8 #> 6 1_1            137.    -71.8 plot_transects(   prepared,   transects,   color_by = "transect_id",   show_legend = FALSE,   contour_interval = 25,   title = "Terrain-Oriented Transects" ) plot_cross_sections(   cross_sections,   value_col = "bathy_m",   show_legend = TRUE,   mean_profile = TRUE,   mean_profile_na_rm = TRUE,   normalize_distance = FALSE,   profile_direction = "top_to_bottom",   title = "Bathymetric Cross-Sections",   subtitle = "Profiles read from shallow to deep terrain" ) one_transect <- cross_sections[   cross_sections$transect_id == cross_sections$transect_id[1], ]  plot_depth_profile(   one_transect,   value_col = "bathy_m",   profile_direction = "top_to_bottom",   title = "Bathymetry Along One Transect",   subtitle = "Distance is oriented from shallow to deep terrain" ) metric_samples <- sample_transects(   transects,   c(prepared, terrain[["slope_deg"]]),   n = 25 ) metric_one <- metric_samples[   metric_samples$transect_id == metric_samples$transect_id[1], ]  plot_depth_profile(   metric_one,   depth_col = "bathy_m",   value_col = "slope_deg",   profile_direction = "top_to_bottom",   title = "Slope Along Depth",   subtitle = "Bathymetry is on the y-axis; slope is on the x-axis" )

title: Get started with blueterra
text: Isobath corridors summarize terrain along depth horizons. source isobaths shown black reader can see contour corridor buffers. Corridors use 5 m buffer around source isobath.
code: isobaths <- extract_isobaths(prepared, depths = c(-50, -80, -120)) corridors <- make_isobath_corridors(prepared, depths = c(-50, -80, -120), width = 5)  corridors[, c("contour_value", "depth_label", "corridor_id")] #> class       : SpatVector #> geometry    : polygons #> dimensions  : 3, 3  (geometries, attributes) #> extent      : 137471.2, 137777, 205664.4, 205761.6  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : NAD83 / Puerto Rico & Virgin Is. (EPSG:32161) #> names       : contour_value depth_label corridor_id #> type        :         <num>       <num>       <int> #> values      :           -50         -50           1 #>                         -80         -80           2 #>                        -120        -120           3 summarize_isobath_terrain(terrain, corridors)[, c("contour_value", "slope_deg_mean", "bpi_3x3_mean")] #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   contour_value slope_deg_mean bpi_3x3_mean #>           <dbl>          <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1           -50           46.2     -0.01000 #> 2           -80           40.0     -0.0209  #> 3          -120           77.7      1.25 plot_isobath_corridors(   corridors,   prepared,   isobaths = isobaths,   background_contours = FALSE,   title = "Isobath Corridors and Source Isobaths",   subtitle = "Corridors use a 5 m buffer around each source isobath" )

title: Get started with blueterra
text: Project-specific metrics can added share grid geometry terrain stack. expression combines local gradient relief variability compact demonstration index.
code: slope_tri <- derive_custom_metric(   terrain,   name = "slope_tri_index",   expression = quote(slope_deg * tri) )  extended_terrain <- add_metric_layers(terrain, slope_tri) names(extended_terrain) #>  [1] "slope_deg"          "aspect_deg"         "northness"          #>  [4] "eastness"           "tri"                "rugosity_vrm_3x3"   #>  [7] "bpi_3x3"            "bpi_11x11"          "curvature"          #> [10] "surface_area_ratio" "slope_tri_index" plot_metric(   extended_terrain,   metric = "slope_tri_index",   bathy = prepared,   contours = TRUE,   contour_interval = 25,   title = "Custom Slope-TRI Index" )

title: Custom metrics and process groups
text: Functions useful metric requires several operations parameters. function must return single-layer terra::SpatRaster grid.
code: relief_position <- derive_custom_metric(   metrics,   name = "relief_position_index",   fun = function(r, bpi_weight = 2) {     out <- r[["tri"]] + bpi_weight * abs(r[["bpi_3x3"]])     names(out) <- "relief_position_index"     out   },   bpi_weight = 1.5 )  relief_position #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 37, 75, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 3.996743, 3.996743  (x, y) #> extent      : 137474.2, 137774, 205626.5, 205774.3  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : NAD83 / Puerto Rico & Virgin Is. (EPSG:32161) #> source(s)   : memory #> varname     : laparguera_hitw_bathy #> name        : relief_position_index #> min value   :              1.158563 #> max value   :             26.458204

title: Custom metrics and process groups
text: add_metric_layers() checks CRS, extent, resolution, dimensions, origin default. protects later summaries PCA tables mixing non-aligned rasters.
code: extended <- add_metric_layers(metrics, slope_tri, relief_position) names(extended) #> [1] "slope_deg"             "tri"                   "bpi_3x3"               #> [4] "bpi_11x11"             "curvature"             "slope_tri_index"       #> [7] "relief_position_index" plot_metric(   extended,   "slope_tri_index",   bathy = prepared,   contours = TRUE,   contour_interval = 25,   title = "Custom Slope-TRI Index" )

title: Custom metrics and process groups

title: Terrain metrics
text: Slope aspect describe local orientation. Aspect circular, northness eastness convert two linear components can summarized tables used predictors.
code: orientation <- derive_metric_stack(   prepared,   metrics = c("slope", "aspect", "northness", "eastness") ) names(orientation) #> [1] "slope_deg"  "aspect_deg" "northness"  "eastness" terra::global(orientation[["slope_deg"]], c("min", "mean", "max"), na.rm = TRUE) #>                min     mean      max #> slope_deg 10.63308 50.87477 81.67002 plot_metric(   orientation,   "slope_deg",   bathy = prepared,   contours = TRUE,   contour_interval = 25,   title = "Slope Over Hillshade" )

what: Roughness, TRI, Rugosity, and Surface Area
title: Terrain metrics
text: metrics describe local relief variability. values change grid resolution focal-window size, windows chosen match feature scale interpreted.  Surface-area ratio derived slope interpreted local raster-cell surface approximation, measured benthic surface area.
code: structure_metrics <- derive_metric_stack(   prepared,   metrics = c("roughness", "tri", "rugosity", "surface_area_ratio") ) names(structure_metrics) #> [1] "roughness"          "tri"                "rugosity_vrm_3x3"   #> [4] "surface_area_ratio" terra::global(structure_metrics[["tri"]], c("min", "mean", "max"), na.rm = TRUE) #>          min     mean      max #> tri 1.047873 4.929997 21.57846 terra::global(structure_metrics[["surface_area_ratio"]], c("min", "mean", "max"), na.rm = TRUE) #>                         min     mean      max #> surface_area_ratio 1.017471 1.954173 6.902548 plot_metric(   structure_metrics,   "rugosity_vrm_3x3",   bathy = prepared,   contours = TRUE,   contour_interval = 25,   title = "Rugosity Over Hillshade" ) plot_metric(   structure_metrics,   "surface_area_ratio",   bathy = prepared,   contours = TRUE,   contour_interval = 25,   title = "Surface-Area Ratio Over Hillshade" )

title: Terrain metrics
text: BPI TPI compare cell neighborhood. elevation-like negative bathymetry, positive BPI means cell higher stored value neighborhood, usually means shallower terrain. positive-depth rasters, interpretation reverses unless sign convention converted first.
code: fine_bpi <- derive_bpi(prepared, window = 3) broad_bpi <- derive_bpi(prepared, window = 11) tpi <- derive_tpi(prepared) multi_bpi <- derive_multiscale_bpi(prepared, windows = c(3, 7, 11))  names(multi_bpi) #> [1] "bpi_3x3"   "bpi_7x7"   "bpi_11x11" terra::global(fine_bpi, c("min", "mean", "max"), na.rm = TRUE) #>               min        mean      max #> bpi_3x3 -5.548068 0.005683195 5.581367 terra::global(broad_bpi, c("min", "mean", "max"), na.rm = TRUE) #>              min        mean      max #> bpi_11x11 -24.95 -0.02859849 29.15818 terra::global(tpi, c("min", "mean", "max"), na.rm = TRUE) #>           min        mean      max #> tpi -6.241576 -0.01349438 6.279038 plot_metric(   fine_bpi,   bathy = prepared,   contours = TRUE,   contour_interval = 25,   title = "Fine-Scale BPI Over Hillshade" )

title: Terrain metrics

title: Transects and cross-sections
text: sample_transects() extracts raster values regular positions along line. extract_cross_sections() alias operation output used profile table.  value column explicit. matters transect tables include numeric metadata width_m, height_m, angle_deg, offset. default profile direction places top shallow endpoint left bottom deeper endpoint right. negative-elevation bathymetry, means profiles read higher numeric values toward lower numeric values. Empty profile ends trimmed distance reset zero plotting. mean profile uses mean_profile_na_rm = TRUE default, continues across full available distance range instead stopping shortest transect ends.
code: samples <- sample_transects(transects, prepared, n = 12) sections <- extract_cross_sections(transects, prepared, n = 12)  head(samples[, c("transect_id", "distance", "x", "y", "bathy_m")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   transect_id distance       x       y bathy_m #>   <chr>          <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 1_1              0   137762. 205591.   NaN   #> 2 1_1             27.4 137760. 205618.   NaN   #> 3 1_1             54.7 137758. 205646.   NaN   #> 4 1_1             82.1 137755. 205673.  -197.  #> 5 1_1            109.  137753. 205700.   -94.8 #> 6 1_1            137.  137751. 205727.   -71.8 summarize_cross_sections(samples, value_col = "bathy_m") #> # A tibble: 4 × 6 #>   transect_id bathy_m_mean bathy_m_sd bathy_m_min bathy_m_max bathy_m_median #>   <chr>              <dbl>      <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 1_1                -102.       66.1       -197.       -45.4          -83.3 #> 2 1_2                -108.       61.2       -192.       -50.7          -95.1 #> 3 1_3                -127.       71.9       -216.       -46.5         -107.  #> 4 1_4                -110.       71.7       -209.       -37.3          -83.7 identical(names(samples), names(sections)) #> [1] TRUE plot_cross_sections(   samples,   value_col = "bathy_m",   show_legend = TRUE,   mean_profile = TRUE,   mean_profile_na_rm = TRUE,   normalize_distance = FALSE,   profile_direction = "top_to_bottom",   title = "Bathymetric Cross-Sections",   subtitle = "Profiles read from shallow to deep terrain" )

title: blueterra
text: compact workflow reads Hole---Wall bathymetry, checks raster assumptions, prepares surface, derives focused terrain stack, summarizes metrics inside sampling rectangle. Depth sign conventions preserved unless conversion requested explicitly. example rasters stored negative elevation, larger numeric values shallower smaller values deeper.
code: bathy_info(hitw) #> # A tibble: 1 × 13 #>   layer    nrow  ncol ncell    xmin   xmax   ymin   ymax  xres  yres   min   max #>   <chr>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 bathy_m    75    75  5625 137474. 1.38e5 2.06e5 2.06e5  4.00  4.00 -269. -16.6 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: crs <chr>  hitw_prepared <- prepare_bathy(   hitw,   depth_range = c(-220, -25),   smooth = TRUE,   smooth_window = 3 )  hitw_metrics <- derive_terrain(   hitw_prepared,   metrics = c("slope", "aspect", "northness", "eastness", "tri", "rugosity",               "bpi", "curvature", "surface_area_ratio") )  terrain_summary <- summarize_terrain(   hitw_metrics,   hitw_rect,   fun = c("mean", "sd", "min", "max") )  names(hitw_metrics) #>  [1] "slope_deg"          "aspect_deg"         "northness"          #>  [4] "eastness"           "tri"                "rugosity_vrm_3x3"   #>  [7] "bpi_3x3"            "bpi_11x11"          "curvature"          #> [10] "surface_area_ratio" terrain_summary[, c("site_id", "site_name", "slope_deg_mean", "bpi_3x3_mean")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   site_id site_name        slope_deg_mean bpi_3x3_mean #>   <chr>   <chr>                     <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 hitw    Hole-in-the-Wall           50.9      0.00568

code: plot_cross_sections(   samples,   value_col = NULL,   group_col = "transect_id",   color_col = NULL,   show_legend = TRUE,   points = FALSE,   mean_profile = FALSE,   mean_profile_na_rm = TRUE,   normalize_distance = FALSE,   profile_direction = c("top_to_bottom", "bottom_to_top", "as_sampled", "max_to_min",     "min_to_max", "high_to_low", "low_to_high"),   positive_depth = NULL,   depth_increases_down = TRUE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL )

title: Plot sampled cross-sections — plot_cross_sections
text: samples Output sample_transects(). value_col Optional value column. group_col Grouping column transect lines. color_col Optional column used color profiles. Defaults group_col. show_legend Logical. Show line-color legend. points Logical. Draw sample points profile lines. mean_profile Logical. Overlay interpolated mean profile across transects. mean_profile_na_rm Logical. Remove missing interpolated profile values averaging mean profile. default, TRUE, lets mean profile use full available distance range rather stopping shortest transect ends. Set FALSE require every profile contribute distance. normalize_distance Logical. Plot distance 0-1 normalized position along transect. profile_direction Direction used orient distance plotting. "top_to_bottom" (default) orients bathymetric elevation profiles shallow top endpoint toward deeper bottom endpoint. negative-elevation bathymetry means higher numeric values lower numeric values. positive-depth bathymetry, set positive_depth = TRUE profile runs lower depth values higher depth values. "bottom_to_top" reverses convention. "max_to_min" "min_to_max" provide explicit numeric endpoint controls metrics, "as_sampled" preserves sampled line order. Legacy values "high_to_low" "low_to_high" accepted aliases "top_to_bottom" "bottom_to_top". positive_depth Logical depth convention value_col. affects top--bottom profile orientation depth-like variables y-axis display positive-depth values. depth_increases_down Logical. TRUE, positive-depth profiles plotted reversed y-axis larger depths appear lower panel. title, subtitle, caption Plot text.

title: blueterra 0.1.0
text: Initial development version. Added raster input, validation, preparation, terrain metrics, process groups, transects, isobath corridors, summaries, modeling helpers, plotting helpers, synthetic example data, tests, README, vignettes. Added reduced real bathymetry examples southwest Puerto Rico shelf margin near La Parguera, Puerto Rico, plus sampling rectangles. Improved hillshade-supported terrain maps, site-location documentation, scientific examples README, vignettes, visual proof. Added surface-oriented transect defaults, richer cross-section PCA plots, citation metadata, custom metric/process-group extension helpers.
